query: If I don't pass a STEOP exam, how many more tries do I have before I'm out?

queries: If I don't pass a STEOP exam, how many more tries do I have before I'm out?

retrieved.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
retrieved.text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

retrieved.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
retrieved.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

retrieved.title: FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity)
heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
retrieved.text: exams easier to ensure that students take a certain number of exams?esenkt und die Prüfungen leichter? No. The performance requirements are not changed at all. Your performance continues to count. - What happens if I do not take a certain number of exams? If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. If you no longer actively study one or several of your degree programmes, you can [close the relevant degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programmes/). - Please also notify the University if you [discontinue exams or courses for an important reason](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/). This gives other students the opportunity to take a spot on the course or in the exam. - Which support measures help me to be ‘prüfungsaktiv’ (take a certain number of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/)

The fourth attempt takes place orally before an examination committee. Please register to an examination in front of an examination committee at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). If the fourth examination attempt was not successful, you will be permanently blocked from this degree programme and any other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, for which the same examination result is required. Admission to these degree programmes is not possible any more. Course with continuous assessment In the event of a negative grade, you will have to repeat the entire course with continuous assessment. You can repeat a course offered by a different course instructor if it serves the same purpose with regard to your curriculum. The course can be repeated three times (four attempts in total). If the fourth attempt was not successful, you will be deregistered from this degree programme at the University of Vienna (and any other degree programmes for which this course is required). A resumption of the study programme is not possible. retrieved.text: grade in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your transcript of records. You can re-sit a passed exam once within twelve months (provided that you have not yet completed your degree programme/stage of the degree programme/module). With your new attempt, the previous examination result becomes void and the new grade is entered, even if the second grade is lower than the first. If no courses with continuous assessment are offered in the semester following your first attempt, you can only re-sit the exam in the semester after next at the latest, but not after you have completed the corresponding module, stage of the degree programme or degree programme. You should announce that you would like to re-sit the exam within 6 months. If you do not use this opportunity, you will not be able to repeat the exam at a later point. Related entries: - [Proof of academic success ("Studienerfolgsnachweis")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31276&cHash=67d9bdbca177eb0e3f60926ca3882521) - Postgraduate Center The University of Vienna's Postgraduate Center offers the possibility of continuing education and training by means of continuing education programmes and certificate courses. retrieved.title: Additional exams
retrieved.text: already completed the STEOP of your current degree programme. The successful completion is a valid proof of the additional exam for the admission to another degree programme. - Where can I pass the additional exam outside of the University of Vienna? If you have to meet the additional requirements prior to admission but cannot prove the corresponding knowledge by means of your school reports, you will have to take your additional exam outside of the University of Vienna. Additional exams can be passed at a [Bildungsdirektion](https://www.bmbwf.gv.at/Themen/schule/schulsystem/bdir.html) in Austria.Please contact the School Board in charge. In case you provide a proof of Latin or Greek by other means, contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) of the Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/) in order to clarify whether or not your proof will be accepted.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. Related Links: - [Re-enrolment](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Continuation of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31960&cHash=051426adf043f8ab058f4e6c4520bb53) - Re-sitting an exam It is possible to re-sit a STEOP exam three times. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa)

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

retrieved.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
retrieved.text:
The admission to master programmes is possible with supplementary examinations to an extent of up to 30 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. If this extent is exceeded, your application for admission will be rejected.

Your application for admission was rejected for this reason? You can make up for lacking supplementary examinations in a non-degree programme, until 30 ECTS or less remain. For this purpose, you will be admitted to "attending individual courses", not to a specific degree programme.

For example: You lack 39 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. Therefore, your application is rejected. Subsequently, you complete supplementary examinations to an extent of 9 ECTS or more as a non-degree student.  Afterwards, you can apply for admission to the degree programme again within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

If the supplementary examination in German was prescribed in your admission letter, please read the information regarding the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) carefully.

- valid passport or personal ID card in original form

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: required). A resumption of the study programme is not possible. Appeal against a negative assessment You can appeal against a negative grade if there were serious irregularities in the conduct of the exam (e.g. reduced time, noise, disturbance, change in mode of assessment that was not announced). You cannot appeal against the content-related assessment as this is at the examiner's discretion. In the event of serious irregularities at the time of the exam, you can apply for a deletion of your grade/attempt in the Office of the Studienpräses within four weeks after grading. The plausibility of the serious irregularities has to be demonstrated. A proof is not necessary. You will receive an official notice, notifying you whether or not your application was successful. Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses (in German)](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa) - Non-degree programme A non-degree programme means that you are admitted to “attending individual courses”. In this case, you do not study any particular degree programme. Especially persons who have to complete supplementary exams as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (Vorstudienlehrgang der Wiener Universitäten, VWU) are admitted to a non-degree programme. As part of the university entrance qualification examination(Studienberechtigungsprüfung – studying without a secondary-school leaving certificate), non-degree programme students can also complete courses. Related Links: - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/) - [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna

retrieved.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text:
You have started and interrupted a bachelor's or diploma programme with entrance exam/aptitude test procedure at the University of Vienna? If so, you are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the same programme. This is also the case if there was no entrance exam procedure at the time.

**Follow these steps:**

- Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact) within the [deadline](#c4319).
- Select the topic “Bachelor/diploma programmes”.
- State which programme you would like to resume.
- Attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card.

i

There are **no exemptions from the entrance exam procedure** in the bachelor's programme in Psychology. Apply for admission within the [application period for degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/deadlines-and-dates) and participate in the written test. Resuming your studies is only possible if you are successful in the entrance exam.

An exemption is in place for certain groups of degree programmes: If you are already admitted to a programme at the University of Vienna and want to study an additional programme of or change your programme within that group you do not have to participate in the entrance exam procedure again.

This rule only applies the the University of Vienna's programmes listed below, and not to students of other universities.

©
Ian Ehm

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
retrieved.text:
If you study full-time, you are expected to obtain 120 ECTS credits within four semesters according to the curriculum. The majority of students achieves 16 ECTS credits within one semester, for example by completing the introductory and orientation period (STEOP).

There is no mandatory study pace apart from the minimum number of credits. You decide yourself how fast you study and how many ECTS credits you complete per semester. However, please note that financial aids and scholarships may be subject to stricter rules.

The minimum number of credits does not apply to

- students with proven physical/mental impairments
- bachelor’s or diploma programmes to which you have been admitted before the winter semester of 2022/23
- master’s and doctoral programmes

Information about the [recognition of the additional exam Latin](https://klassischephilologie.univie.ac.at/studium/ergaenzungspruefungen/latein-ergaenzungspruefung/) for the minimum number of credits can be found (in German only) on the website of the Institute for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

retrieved.text: to pass, before I submit my application for admission? Unfortunately, this is not possible as which and if any supplementary examinations are prescribed will only be determined individually during the processing of your documents. - I already have a university degree and now want to study a bachelor/diploma programme. Can I apply with this certificate instead of my secondary-school leaving certificate? Yes, if you have completed a programme with a duration of at least three years or at least 180 ECTS (e.g. a three-year bachelor programme) at a recognised post-secondary educational institution you can use this as proof of your university entrance qualification instead of your secondary-school leaving certificate.

retrieved.title: Additional exams
retrieved.text:
For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Greek has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Greek at a secondary school after the 8th grade for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission.

- Prior to admission:
  For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Greek at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) about your options.
- During the degree programme:
  For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Greek during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes).

If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet.

The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology. You get all information at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/studium/zusatz-und-ergaenzungspruefungen/).

retrieved.text: study places. [Information about the registration system and the registration deadlines](/en/semester-planning/registration-for-coursesexaminations/) - Step 5: Complete courses The first examination date for a course with non-continuous assessment will take place after the last unit of the semester. Subsequently, the university will offer three additional examination dates. The fourth examination attempt has to take place before an examination committee. Courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars, practical courses) do not end with a final examination, but rather require you to complete two or more oral or written partial achievements. These partial achievements are the basis for determining your final grade. You can find your grade in the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass).

retrieved.title: FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity)
heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
retrieved.text: of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/) Make use of the [services for students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) to achieve your goal. The University supports you by offering: - In your first semester: [STEOP mentoring](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/your-first-semester/steop-mentoring/) - Reflect on, and adapt, your own learning approach: [Learning camp](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/learning-camp/) - Improve your academic writing for paper or thesis: [Writing mentoring](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/writing-mentoring/), [writing consultation](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/writing-consultation/) - Writing theses: [research question camp](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/research-question-camp/), [methods camp](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/methods-camp/), [writing days](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/writing-days/) - In selected fields of study: [Peer Student-Counseling](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/peer-advice-on-study-progress/) - [Helpful handouts and blog posts (german)](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/ressourcen/handouts-leitfaeden-und-infomaterial/) As usual, we keep you informed by e-mail, on the websites and via the social media channels of the University of Vienna. - Why are SWS sometimes displayed in u:space? To be considered examination-active, you must achieve at least 16 ECTS credits or at least 8 SWS (semester hours per week). Some students see SWS instead of ECTS credits in u:space. This occurs when SWS are more beneficial for reaching examination activity status more quickly.

reranked.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
reranked.text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

reranked.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
reranked.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

reranked.title: FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity)
heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
reranked.text: exams easier to ensure that students take a certain number of exams?esenkt und die Prüfungen leichter? No. The performance requirements are not changed at all. Your performance continues to count. - What happens if I do not take a certain number of exams? If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. If you no longer actively study one or several of your degree programmes, you can [close the relevant degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programmes/). - Please also notify the University if you [discontinue exams or courses for an important reason](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/). This gives other students the opportunity to take a spot on the course or in the exam. - Which support measures help me to be ‘prüfungsaktiv’ (take a certain number of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/)

You will receive an official notice informing you about whether or not your application was successful. reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Negative assessment Exam In the event of a negative grade, you will be able to re-sit an exam three times, which means that you can take an exam four times in total. The fourth attempt takes place orally before an examination committee. Please register to an examination in front of an examination committee at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). If the fourth examination attempt was not successful, you will be permanently blocked from this degree programme and any other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, for which the same examination result is required. Admission to these degree programmes is not possible any more. Course with continuous assessment In the event of a negative grade, you will have to repeat the entire course with continuous assessment. You can repeat a course offered by a different course instructor if it serves the same purpose with regard to your curriculum. The course can be repeated three times (four attempts in total). If the fourth attempt was not successful, you will be deregistered from this degree programme at the University of Vienna (and any other degree programmes for which this course is required). A resumption of the study programme is not possible. reranked.text: grade in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your transcript of records. You can re-sit a passed exam once within twelve months (provided that you have not yet completed your degree programme/stage of the degree programme/module). With your new attempt, the previous examination result becomes void and the new grade is entered, even if the second grade is lower than the first. If no courses with continuous assessment are offered in the semester following your first attempt, you can only re-sit the exam in the semester after next at the latest, but not after you have completed the corresponding module, stage of the degree programme or degree programme. You should announce that you would like to re-sit the exam within 6 months. If you do not use this opportunity, you will not be able to repeat the exam at a later point. Related entries: - [Proof of academic success ("Studienerfolgsnachweis")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31276&cHash=67d9bdbca177eb0e3f60926ca3882521) - Postgraduate Center The University of Vienna's Postgraduate Center offers the possibility of continuing education and training by means of continuing education programmes and certificate courses. For the participation in a continuing education and training programmes, a tuition fee is charged. Related Links: - [Postgraduate Center](https://www.postgraduatecenter.at/en/) Related entries: - [University continuing education and training programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31320&cHash=55408351546b9c990256e179042cc5cd) - Practical course (course type) Related entries: - [Course with non-continuous assessment (npi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31201&cHash=60e218c8de231c88ec12793220a06022) - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56)

reranked.text: already completed the STEOP of your current degree programme. The successful completion is a valid proof of the additional exam for the admission to another degree programme. - Where can I pass the additional exam outside of the University of Vienna? If you have to meet the additional requirements prior to admission but cannot prove the corresponding knowledge by means of your school reports, you will have to take your additional exam outside of the University of Vienna. Additional exams can be passed at a [Bildungsdirektion](https://www.bmbwf.gv.at/Themen/schule/schulsystem/bdir.html) in Austria.Please contact the School Board in charge. In case you provide a proof of Latin or Greek by other means, contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) of the Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/) in order to clarify whether or not your proof will be accepted.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. Related Links: - [Re-enrolment](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Continuation of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31960&cHash=051426adf043f8ab058f4e6c4520bb53) - Re-sitting an exam It is possible to re-sit a STEOP exam three times. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa)

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

reranked.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
reranked.text:
The admission to master programmes is possible with supplementary examinations to an extent of up to 30 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. If this extent is exceeded, your application for admission will be rejected.

Your application for admission was rejected for this reason? You can make up for lacking supplementary examinations in a non-degree programme, until 30 ECTS or less remain. For this purpose, you will be admitted to "attending individual courses", not to a specific degree programme.

For example: You lack 39 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. Therefore, your application is rejected. Subsequently, you complete supplementary examinations to an extent of 9 ECTS or more as a non-degree student.  Afterwards, you can apply for admission to the degree programme again within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

If the supplementary examination in German was prescribed in your admission letter, please read the information regarding the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) carefully.

- valid passport or personal ID card in original form

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

reranked.text: required). A resumption of the study programme is not possible. Appeal against a negative assessment You can appeal against a negative grade if there were serious irregularities in the conduct of the exam (e.g. reduced time, noise, disturbance, change in mode of assessment that was not announced). You cannot appeal against the content-related assessment as this is at the examiner's discretion. In the event of serious irregularities at the time of the exam, you can apply for a deletion of your grade/attempt in the Office of the Studienpräses within four weeks after grading. The plausibility of the serious irregularities has to be demonstrated. A proof is not necessary. You will receive an official notice, notifying you whether or not your application was successful. Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses (in German)](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa) - Non-degree programme A non-degree programme means that you are admitted to “attending individual courses”. In this case, you do not study any particular degree programme. Especially persons who have to complete supplementary exams as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (Vorstudienlehrgang der Wiener Universitäten, VWU) are admitted to a non-degree programme. As part of the university entrance qualification examination(Studienberechtigungsprüfung – studying without a secondary-school leaving certificate), non-degree programme students can also complete courses. Related Links: - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/) - [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna

reranked.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
reranked.text:
You have started and interrupted a bachelor's or diploma programme with entrance exam/aptitude test procedure at the University of Vienna? If so, you are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the same programme. This is also the case if there was no entrance exam procedure at the time.

**Follow these steps:**

- Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact) within the [deadline](#c4319).
- Select the topic “Bachelor/diploma programmes”.
- State which programme you would like to resume.
- Attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card.

i

There are **no exemptions from the entrance exam procedure** in the bachelor's programme in Psychology. Apply for admission within the [application period for degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/deadlines-and-dates) and participate in the written test. Resuming your studies is only possible if you are successful in the entrance exam.

An exemption is in place for certain groups of degree programmes: If you are already admitted to a programme at the University of Vienna and want to study an additional programme of or change your programme within that group you do not have to participate in the entrance exam procedure again.

This rule only applies the the University of Vienna's programmes listed below, and not to students of other universities.

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
reranked.text:
If you study full-time, you are expected to obtain 120 ECTS credits within four semesters according to the curriculum. The majority of students achieves 16 ECTS credits within one semester, for example by completing the introductory and orientation period (STEOP).

There is no mandatory study pace apart from the minimum number of credits. You decide yourself how fast you study and how many ECTS credits you complete per semester. However, please note that financial aids and scholarships may be subject to stricter rules.

The minimum number of credits does not apply to

- students with proven physical/mental impairments
- bachelor’s or diploma programmes to which you have been admitted before the winter semester of 2022/23
- master’s and doctoral programmes

Information about the [recognition of the additional exam Latin](https://klassischephilologie.univie.ac.at/studium/ergaenzungspruefungen/latein-ergaenzungspruefung/) for the minimum number of credits can be found (in German only) on the website of the Institute for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

reranked.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
reranked.text: to pass, before I submit my application for admission? Unfortunately, this is not possible as which and if any supplementary examinations are prescribed will only be determined individually during the processing of your documents. - I already have a university degree and now want to study a bachelor/diploma programme. Can I apply with this certificate instead of my secondary-school leaving certificate? Yes, if you have completed a programme with a duration of at least three years or at least 180 ECTS (e.g. a three-year bachelor programme) at a recognised post-secondary educational institution you can use this as proof of your university entrance qualification instead of your secondary-school leaving certificate.

reranked.title: Additional exams
reranked.text:
For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Greek has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Greek at a secondary school after the 8th grade for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission.

- Prior to admission:
  For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Greek at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) about your options.
- During the degree programme:
  For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Greek during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes).

If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet.

The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology. You get all information at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/studium/zusatz-und-ergaenzungspruefungen/).

reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text: study places. [Information about the registration system and the registration deadlines](/en/semester-planning/registration-for-coursesexaminations/) - Step 5: Complete courses The first examination date for a course with non-continuous assessment will take place after the last unit of the semester. Subsequently, the university will offer three additional examination dates. The fourth examination attempt has to take place before an examination committee. Courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars, practical courses) do not end with a final examination, but rather require you to complete two or more oral or written partial achievements. These partial achievements are the basis for determining your final grade. You can find your grade in the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass).

reranked.title: FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity)
heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
reranked.text: of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/) Make use of the [services for students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) to achieve your goal. The University supports you by offering: - In your first semester: [STEOP mentoring](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/your-first-semester/steop-mentoring/) - Reflect on, and adapt, your own learning approach: [Learning camp](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/learning-camp/) - Improve your academic writing for paper or thesis: [Writing mentoring](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/writing-mentoring/), [writing consultation](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/writing-consultation/) - Writing theses: [research question camp](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/research-question-camp/), [methods camp](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/methods-camp/), [writing days](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/writing-days/) - In selected fields of study: [Peer Student-Counseling](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/during-the-degree-programme/peer-advice-on-study-progress/) - [Helpful handouts and blog posts (german)](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/angebote-fuer-studierende/ressourcen/handouts-leitfaeden-und-infomaterial/) As usual, we keep you informed by e-mail, on the websites and via the social media channels of the University of Vienna. - Why are SWS sometimes displayed in u:space? To be considered examination-active, you must achieve at least 16 ECTS credits or at least 8 SWS (semester hours per week). Some students see SWS instead of ECTS credits in u:space. This occurs when SWS are more beneficial for reaching examination activity status more quickly.

selected.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
selected.text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

selected.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
selected.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

selected.title: FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity)
heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?

Your performance continues to count. - What happens if I do not take a certain number of exams? If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. If you no longer actively study one or several of your degree programmes, you can [close the relevant degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programmes/). - Please also notify the University if you [discontinue exams or courses for an important reason](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/). This gives other students the opportunity to take a spot on the course or in the exam. - Which support measures help me to be ‘prüfungsaktiv’ (take a certain number of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/)

selected.text: your transcript of records. This counts as one of your permissible attempts. You will have lost one examination attempt. If you state an appropriate reason, you can apply for the deletion of your examination attempt in the Office of the Studienpräses within 14 days after your exam has been marked with an “X”. You will receive an official notice informing you about whether or not your application was successful. Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Discontinuing an exam/course with continuous assessment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30167&cHash=040ff36417e980db488e9aba95efa9ef)

If the fourth examination attempt was not successful, you will be permanently blocked from this degree programme and any other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, for which the same examination result is required. Admission to these degree programmes is not possible any more. You can repeat a course offered by a different course instructor if it serves the same purpose with regard to your curriculum. The course can be repeated three times (four attempts in total). If the fourth attempt was not successful, you will be deregistered from this degree programme at the University of Vienna (and any other degree programmes for which this course is required). assemble.context:
[1] Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

[2] Introductory and orientation period (STEOP) — Important Information
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

[3] FAQs about being 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination activity) — Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
exams easier to ensure that students take a certain number of exams?esenkt und die Prüfungen leichter? No. The performance requirements are not changed at all. Your performance continues to count. - What happens if I do not take a certain number of exams? If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. If you no longer actively study one or several of your degree programmes, you can [close the relevant degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programmes/). - Please also notify the University if you [discontinue exams or courses for an important reason](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/). This gives other students the opportunity to take a spot on the course or in the exam. - Which support measures help me to be ‘prüfungsaktiv’ (take a certain number of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/)

[4] ABC of terminology — U
your transcript of records. This counts as one of your permissible attempts. You will have lost one examination attempt. If you state an appropriate reason, you can apply for the deletion of your examination attempt in the Office of the Studienpräses within 14 days after your exam has been marked with an “X”. You will receive an official notice informing you about whether or not your application was successful. Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Discontinuing an exam/course with continuous assessment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30167&cHash=040ff36417e980db488e9aba95efa9ef)

[5] ABC of terminology — N
/ - Negative assessment Exam In the event of a negative grade, you will be able to re-sit an exam three times, which means that you can take an exam four times in total. The fourth attempt takes place orally before an examination committee. Please register to an examination in front of an examination committee at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). If the fourth examination attempt was not successful, you will be permanently blocked from this degree programme and any other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, for which the same examination result is required. Admission to these degree programmes is not possible any more. Course with continuous assessment In the event of a negative grade, you will have to repeat the entire course with continuous assessment. You can repeat a course offered by a different course instructor if it serves the same purpose with regard to your curriculum. The course can be repeated three times (four attempts in total). If the fourth attempt was not successful, you will be deregistered from this degree programme at the University of Vienna (and any other degree programmes for which this course is required). A resumption of the study programme is not possible. Appeal against a negative assessment You can appeal against a negative grade if there were serious irregularities in the conduct of the exam (e.g. reduced time, noise, disturbance, change in

[To the contact form](/en/contact)

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Question: If I don't pass a STEOP exam, how many more tries do I have before I'm out?

generate.raw_response: You have 3 more tries before you're out [1][2][5].

answer: You have 3 more tries before you're out [1][2][5].
